channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Trump to slap 'sweeping' new tariffs on 60 trade partners as global duties expire
item.description: The Trump administration has ramped its tariff actions back up since the president's trade agenda suffered major legal setbacks earlier this year.

item.title: Intel's stock jumps as chipmaker rides AI boom to fastest revenue growth in almost 15 years
item.description: Intel rallied after the company reported blowout earnings and guidance.

item.title: Oracle signs 10-year software contract with Pentagon worth up to $7 billion
item.description: Oracle will supply on-premises software to the U.S. Department of Defense.

item.title: Shortsighted stock market can no longer brush off war: 'It's too hard to ignore $100 oil'
item.description: After staying flat as the U.S.-Iran war heated up again, equities tumbled on Thursday after oil prices crossed $100 per barrel.

item.title: Josh Brown says the stock market is ‘broadening out.' Here’s how to play it
item.description: A few insurance and industrial stocks could offer investors strong returns as the market broadens out beyond the AI trade, according to Josh Brown.

item.title: OpenAI's Hugging Face hack triggers 'AI Kill Switch' bill in Congress
item.description: OpenAI disclosed this week that some of its AI models went rogue and hacked into open-source developer platform Hugging Face.

item.title: Tesla, Alphabet lose hundreds of billions in value in post-earnings stock plunge
item.description: Both Tesla and Alphabet signaled higher spending as they invest in artificial intelligence.

item.title: Amazon and Microsoft pivot cloud gaming strategies to target different players
item.description: Amazon is bringing Luna to the Prime Video streaming service, while Microsoft will test an ad-supported streaming of titles that people own.

item.title: 10-year Treasury yield rises to highest since January 2025 as surging oil rekindles inflation fear
item.description: U.S. Treasury yields climbed on Wednesday, following oil prices higher, as traders also reacted to a slump in weekly claims for unemployment insurance.

item.title: Congress splits on war powers resolutions to force Trump to abandon Iran war
item.description: Trump would be nearly certain to veto any measure to curtail his ability to wage war with Iran.

item.title: As Honda CR-V leads U.S. sales, automaker teases new American-built pickup truck
item.description: The future of the Ridgeline midsize pickup truck had been in flux, and the automaker said there will be a temporary production stoppage for the truck.

item.title: AARP to Congress: 'We strongly object to fast-tracking Social Security changes'
item.description: New legislation aims to break the stalemate on overhauling Social Security. AARP, which advocates on senior issues, says it opposes them.

item.title: Southwest Airlines put Texas jet fuel on a boat to LA for the first time amid supply worries
item.description: Southwest hired a ship this spring to send fuel from Houston to Los Angeles in case of supply shortages.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves after hours: Intel, AMD, Boston Beer, Deckers & more
item.description: These are the stocks posting the largest moves after the bell.

item.title: The 10-year Treasury yield could test 5% after its latest spike. Here’s why
item.description: The benchmark yield at 5% would be psychologically significant for the stock market.

item.title: Odds of Federal Reserve rate hike surge as oil prices rip higher
item.description: Investors are increasingly readying for the Federal Reserve to hike interest rates in September.

item.title: Cerebras stock gains on AMD partnership
item.description: Cerebras and AMD agreed to work together, pairing their technology for AI systems.

item.title: DOJ withdraws subpoenas to NY Times reporters in Trump plane probe
item.description: The subpoenas to New York Times reporters were issued by Manhattan U.S. Attorney Jay Clayton, whom Trump has nominated as director of national intelligence.

item.title: IBM's Krishna tries to reassure investors that AI won't disrupt company's software unit
item.description: IBM's CEO highlighted growing mainframe hardware capacity, and said software should "catch back up" within a year.

item.title: A tax break for preserving land has drawn IRS scrutiny. Here’s when it still makes sense
item.description: For wealthy landowners, conservation easements can still make sense if you avoid these IRS red flags.

item.title: Wall Street stumbles as oil surges, and Lilly takes a new path on next-gen obesity drug
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: Are smartphone credit cards worth it? With the Samsung Galaxy Card launch, here's when they make sense for your wallet
item.description: These are great cards for digital wallet purchases, but not as rewarding for everyday spending.

item.title: As the S&P 500 sells off, traders eye key 'risk pivot' level
item.description: Options traders are seeking clues on whether more volatility may be ahead.

item.title: Brent crude crosses $100 after tankers reportedly struck off Saudi Arabia
item.description: Oil prices rose after attacks on ships in the Red Sea, and U.S. President Donald Trump's threatened to bomb Iranian infrastructure.

item.title: House lawmakers grill former JPMorgan executive Jes Staley over Epstein ties
item.description: House oversight Chair James Comer said Jes Staley defended Jeffrey Epstein and encouraged JPMorgan to retain him despite internal red flags.

item.title: Google Cloud CEO Kurian says customers are spending 50% more as segment blows away expectations
item.description: Google Cloud CEO Thomas Kurian told CNBC on Thursday that the company's existing customers are spending "roughly 50% more" than they've already committed.

item.title: JPMorgan report finds dramatic jump in AI-themed ETFs — despite rough quarter
item.description: Wall Street is banking heavily on exchange-traded funds that give investors artificial intelligence exposure, according to JPMorgan Asset Management.

item.title: Jim Cramer says he is 'struggling to have reasons to buy' in this market
item.description: "We just don't have the horses to continue higher right now," CNBC's Jim Cramer said on Thursday.

item.title: Albertsons stock plunges as it says weaker grocery spending will cut into sales and earnings
item.description: Shares of Albertsons sank Thursday after the grocer reported softness in its core business and lowered its full-year outlook as a result.

item.title: 'The Odyssey' is cleaning up for Imax. Expect the momentum to continue
item.description: Nolan's "The Odyssey" is racking up box office dollars for Imax and fueling investor confidence that the company will reach record ticket sales in 2026.
